receptors.name: CB1
receptors.function: Primary psychoactive cannabinoid receptor. Mediates euphoria, appetite, pain modulation, memory.

receptors.name: CB2
receptors.function: Anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory. Key role in pain and inflammation without psychoactive effects.

receptors.name: TRPV1
receptors.function: Vanilloid receptor. Mediates pain sensation, thermoregulation. Activated by capsaicin and some cannabinoids.

receptors.name: 5-HT1A
receptors.function: Serotonin receptor. Mediates anxiety reduction, mood regulation, anti-depressant effects.

receptors.name: PPARgamma
receptors.function: Nuclear receptor. Anti-inflammatory, neuroprotective. CBD is a partial agonist.

receptors.name: GPR55
receptors.function: Putative cannabinoid receptor. Involved in bone density, anxiety, neuroprotection.

canonicalEffects.name: relaxed
canonicalEffects.description: Full-body tension relief mediated by CB1 activation in the central amygdala and GABAergic interneurons, reducing sympathetic nervous system output.

canonicalEffects.name: euphoric
canonicalEffects.description: Intense well-being and pleasure driven by CB1-mediated dopamine release in the mesolimbic pathway (nucleus accumbens and VTA).

canonicalEffects.name: happy
canonicalEffects.description: Elevated mood linked to serotonergic modulation via 5-HT1A partial agonism and CB1-mediated anandamide elevation in prefrontal cortex.

canonicalEffects.name: creative
canonicalEffects.description: Enhanced divergent thinking associated with increased cerebral blood flow to the frontal lobe and CB1-mediated disinhibition of associative networks.

canonicalEffects.name: energetic
canonicalEffects.description: Increased physical and mental vitality through noradrenergic activation and TRPV1-mediated peripheral stimulation, often associated with pinene and limonene terpene profiles.

canonicalEffects.name: focused
canonicalEffects.description: Improved concentration and attentional control via modulation of prefrontal cortex dopamine and norepinephrine signaling, with alpha-pinene terpene contribution to acetylcholinesterase inhibition.

canonicalEffects.name: uplifted
canonicalEffects.description: Mood elevation and optimism through serotonin and dopamine pathway modulation, often associated with limonene-dominant terpene profiles.

canonicalEffects.name: giggly
canonicalEffects.description: Lowered laughter threshold through CB1-mediated disinhibition of social processing circuits in the temporal and prefrontal cortex.

canonicalEffects.name: talkative
canonicalEffects.description: Enhanced social communication via CB1 modulation of dopaminergic pathways in Broca's area and reduced social anxiety through amygdala suppression.

canonicalEffects.name: tingly
canonicalEffects.description: Peripheral sensory stimulation from TRPV1 and CB1 receptor activation on sensory neurons, producing pleasant paresthesia throughout the body.

canonicalEffects.name: aroused
canonicalEffects.description: Heightened sexual arousal through CB1-mediated vasodilation, oxytocin release, and enhanced tactile sensitivity via peripheral CB1/TRPV1 activation.

canonicalEffects.name: hungry
canonicalEffects.description: Appetite stimulation (the 'munchies') driven by CB1 activation in the hypothalamus, enhanced olfactory sensitivity, and ghrelin release from the stomach.

canonicalEffects.name: sleepy
canonicalEffects.description: Sedation and drowsiness mediated by CBN/THC synergy on CB1 receptors, myrcene terpene potentiation, and GABAergic enhancement promoting sleep onset.

canonicalEffects.name: calm
canonicalEffects.description: Mental tranquility and reduced racing thoughts via CB1-mediated suppression of default mode network overactivity and anxiolytic 5-HT1A partial agonism.

canonicalEffects.name: motivated
canonicalEffects.description: Enhanced goal-directed behavior through low-dose CB1 modulation of dopamine release in the prefrontal cortex and nucleus accumbens, increasing reward anticipation.

canonicalEffects.name: meditative
canonicalEffects.description: Enhanced introspective and mindful states through CB1-mediated default mode network modulation and 5-HT1A-mediated reduction of mental chatter.

canonicalEffects.name: body-high
canonicalEffects.description: Diffuse physical euphoria from CB1 and CB2 activation on peripheral sensory neurons combined with TRPV1-mediated warmth and muscle relaxation.

canonicalEffects.name: head-high
canonicalEffects.description: Cerebral psychoactive effects predominantly from CB1 activation in cortical regions, producing altered perception and thought patterns without heavy body sedation.

canonicalEffects.name: spacey
canonicalEffects.description: Mild dissociation and dreamlike cognition from CB1-mediated disruption of hippocampal theta rhythms and altered temporal perception in the prefrontal cortex.

canonicalEffects.name: mouth-watering
canonicalEffects.description: Increased salivation and taste enhancement driven by CB1 activation in gustatory cortex and parasympathetic stimulation of salivary glands, distinct from appetite stimulation.

canonicalEffects.name: dry-mouth
canonicalEffects.description: Reduced salivation (xerostomia) caused by CB1 and CB2 receptor activation in submandibular gland parasympathetic ganglia, inhibiting acetylcholine-mediated saliva production.

canonicalEffects.name: dry-eyes
canonicalEffects.description: Decreased tear production from CB1-mediated inhibition of lacrimal gland secretion and reduced aqueous humor outflow causing intraocular pressure changes.

canonicalEffects.name: dizzy
canonicalEffects.description: Vestibular disruption and orthostatic hypotension from CB1-mediated vasodilation and reduced cerebellar coordination, particularly at higher doses.

canonicalEffects.name: paranoid
canonicalEffects.description: Excessive threat detection from CB1 overstimulation of the amygdala and hippocampus, overwhelming endocannabinoid buffering and triggering hypervigilant pattern matching.

canonicalEffects.name: anxious
canonicalEffects.description: Heightened anxiety from biphasic CB1 response at high doses -- excessive amygdala activation, cortisol release, and disruption of the HPA axis stress response.

canonicalEffects.name: headache
canonicalEffects.description: Cephalgia potentially from rebound vasodilation, dehydration, or terpene sensitivity, with CB1-mediated changes in trigeminal nerve signaling.

canonicalEffects.name: nauseous
canonicalEffects.description: Dose-dependent emetic response from CB1 activation in the area postrema (chemoreceptor trigger zone) and vagal afferents, paradoxically opposite to antiemetic effects at lower doses.

canonicalEffects.name: rapid-heartbeat
canonicalEffects.description: Tachycardia from CB1-mediated sympathetic activation and vagal inhibition, increasing heart rate by 20-50% acutely, particularly in naive users.

canonicalEffects.name: couch-lock
canonicalEffects.description: Extreme sedation and physical immobility from high-dose CB1 activation combined with myrcene terpene potentiation of GABAergic inhibition in motor circuits.

canonicalEffects.name: disoriented
canonicalEffects.description: Spatial and temporal confusion from CB1-mediated disruption of hippocampal place cells and entorhinal grid cells, impairing navigation and time perception.

canonicalEffects.name: fatigued
canonicalEffects.description: Post-use exhaustion from adenosine accumulation following CB1-mediated dopamine depletion and disrupted sleep architecture during cannabis-induced sleep.

canonicalEffects.name: irritable
canonicalEffects.description: Increased irritability from endocannabinoid system rebound after acute use, with reduced GABAergic tone and heightened amygdala reactivity to negative stimuli.

canonicalEffects.name: pain
canonicalEffects.description: Analgesic relief through CB1/CB2-mediated inhibition of nociceptive signaling in dorsal horn, descending pain modulation, and peripheral anti-inflammatory action via TRPV1 desensitization.

canonicalEffects.name: stress
canonicalEffects.description: Stress reduction through CB1-mediated suppression of HPA axis cortisol output and amygdala fear response, with anxiolytic contribution from 5-HT1A partial agonism.

canonicalEffects.name: anxiety
canonicalEffects.description: Anxiolytic effects at low-moderate doses via CB1-mediated enhancement of GABAergic inhibition in basolateral amygdala and CBD-mediated 5-HT1A partial agonism.

canonicalEffects.name: depression
canonicalEffects.description: Antidepressant-like effects from rapid CB1-mediated enhancement of serotonergic and dopaminergic neurotransmission, with endocannabinoid system restoration of hedonic tone.

canonicalEffects.name: insomnia
canonicalEffects.description: Sleep-promoting effects through CBN/THC CB1 agonism increasing adenosine signaling, myrcene-mediated GABA-A potentiation, and reduction of REM sleep latency.

canonicalEffects.name: nausea-relief
canonicalEffects.description: Antiemetic action through CB1 agonism in the dorsal vagal complex and 5-HT3 receptor antagonism, particularly effective for chemotherapy-induced nausea (CINV).

canonicalEffects.name: appetite-loss
canonicalEffects.description: Appetite stimulation for cachexia and wasting syndromes through CB1-mediated ghrelin release and hypothalamic feeding circuit activation, countering appetite loss.

canonicalEffects.name: inflammation
canonicalEffects.description: Anti-inflammatory action through CB2-mediated immune cell modulation, inhibition of NF-kB pro-inflammatory signaling, and reduction of TNF-alpha, IL-6, and other cytokines.

canonicalEffects.name: muscle-spasms
canonicalEffects.description: Antispasmodic relief through CB1-mediated inhibition of excitatory neurotransmission at neuromuscular junctions and central motor circuit regulation, relevant to MS and spasticity.

canonicalEffects.name: seizures
canonicalEffects.description: Anticonvulsant effects primarily from CBD-mediated GPR55 antagonism, TRPV1 desensitization, and enhanced adenosine signaling reducing neuronal hyperexcitability (FDA-approved for Dravet/Lennox-Gastaut).

canonicalEffects.name: ptsd
canonicalEffects.description: PTSD symptom management through CB1-mediated fear extinction in the amygdala-prefrontal circuit, disruption of traumatic memory reconsolidation, and nightmare suppression.

canonicalEffects.name: migraines
canonicalEffects.description: Migraine relief through CB1-mediated inhibition of trigeminal nociception, serotonergic modulation of cortical spreading depression, and TRPV1-mediated CGRP release reduction.

canonicalEffects.name: fatigue-medical
canonicalEffects.description: Management of chronic fatigue through low-dose CB1-mediated dopaminergic stimulation, improved sleep quality, and reduced neuroinflammation contributing to central fatigue.

canonicalEffects.name: eye-pressure
canonicalEffects.description: Intraocular pressure reduction through CB1-mediated increase in aqueous humor outflow and decreased production via ciliary body receptor activation, relevant to glaucoma treatment.

canonicalEffects.name: arthritis
canonicalEffects.description: Joint pain and inflammation relief through CB2-mediated suppression of synovial inflammation, CB1 analgesia in joint nociceptors, and TRPV1 desensitization in arthritic joints.

canonicalEffects.name: fibromyalgia
canonicalEffects.description: Central sensitization management through endocannabinoid deficiency correction, CB1-mediated descending pain inhibition, and improved sleep quality addressing fibromyalgia's multifactorial pathology.

canonicalEffects.name: adhd
canonicalEffects.description: Attention and impulse regulation through CB1-mediated modulation of prefrontal dopamine signaling, with potential normalization of dopamine transporter function in ADHD patients.

canonicalEffects.name: gastrointestinal
canonicalEffects.description: GI symptom relief through CB1/CB2 modulation of enteric nervous system motility, reduction of intestinal inflammation via CB2, and visceral pain suppression relevant to IBS and Crohn's disease.

canonicalEffects.name: bipolar
canonicalEffects.description: Mood stabilization through endocannabinoid system regulation of glutamate/GABA balance, CB1-mediated dampening of manic dopamine surges, and neuroprotective effects during mood episodes.
